Switching to the adaptation mode adds the Adaptation tab to the details of a LADI. Enlarge the tab and choose Edit: You can now select the Adapt checkbox for each property you want and enter your adaptation in the input field behind. Adapted values can be translated using the SE63.

For more information about adapting technical catalogs, please read the blog post of ruth_groene:
SAP Fiori Launchpad: New Fast and Easy Option to Adapt SAP-delivered Launchpad Content (for On-Premise and Cloud, Private Edition)

Key User Adaptation

There are so many other topics around SAP Fiori that there is just not enough room for everything in a 5-day-couse. But one topic was always there as an outlook: Key user adaptation. With key user adaptation or UI adaptation at runtime (RTA), SAP Fiori apps based on SAPUI5 that support RTA can be changed directly and intuitively in the SAP Fiori launchpad without developing any source code. With SAP S/4HANA 2023, versions of such app variants were introduced.

How to Activate and Publish a New Version (Screenshot from UX100)

After the adaptations to an app have been saved in a variant, they can be activated as a new version. This sets this variant as the active version for all users in the system replacing the original app or any previously active version. You can switch to an older version by activating it as a new version.

With the active version being selected, the Publish button can be used to assign it to a transport request. A customizing or workbench request can be selected to decide, if the adaptations should be available client-specific or cross-client in the follow-up system.

For more information about key user adaptation, please read the tutorial of Vitor Seifert:
Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications - UI Changes and Modes

Summary

If you have read so far, it seems that you are really thinking about attending the course. Don’t hesitate and check out the physical and virtual events we offer:

https://training.sap.com/course/UX100

If you are more interested in a digital learning, please visit https://learning.sap.com. Many parts of the UX100 are already available free-of-charge:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journey/learn-the-basics-of-sap-fiori

For any other questions around the offerings of SAP Learning Services, please jump to our community page and get in contact with us:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/training-certification

I hope even if you won’t attend the UX100 in any kind, you found some useful information or references around SAP Fiori in this blog post.

Happy learning
Stefan

entry.title: TIL - How to force a specific version of SAP UI5 when running Fiori preview in a CAP project
entry.content:
Today, I was running a very simple CAP service, which is part of the Service integration with SAP Cloud Application Programming model CodeJam. When running the Fiori preview for an entity, I was always getting an error message in the console: TypeError: o.intersection is not a function. This error was raised by the Fiori preview independent of the entity that I was trying to preview. That made me thing that the issue was not with the entity but with the Fiori preview itself.


Fiori preview error

Based on the network requests that I was able to see in the Developer Tools, I knew it was using version 1.132.0 of SAP UI5. Then I thought… let’s force the project to use a different version to see if the error would go away. I debugged the CAP project and I knew exactly where was the version of SAP UI5 being set. I set the variable to 1.130.0 and the preview worked fine. Nice!


Debug forcing version

Now, I don’t want to debug the project every time and manually set the version…. digging deeper, I found out that the @sap/cds-fiori module has a package.json, where the version of SAP UI5 is set. It is well known that you can set project-specific configuration via a .env file. So, I created a .env file in the root of the project and
set the following content:

cds.fiori.preview.ui5.version=1.130.0

By setting that environment variable I’m forcing the project to use version 1.130.0 of SAP UI5 when running the Fiori preview. I restarted the project and the preview worked fine.


Fiori preview running specific version

Content originally published in https://ajmaradiaga.com/TIL-Force-UI5-version-in-Fiori-preview/

entry.title: Migrate your code with UI5 linter – Autofix has landed
entry.content:
UI5 linter is the tool of choice to check your UI5 project for using best practices and replacement of deprecated UI5 APIs. Last year, we focused on the detection capabilities of UI5 linter. In early 2025, we started on an actual migration functionality to be offered by UI5 linter, a.k.a "Autofix". Right from the beginning, our goal was to provide a similar developer experience as offered by the famous ESLint project. With UI5 linter v1.12.0, we are happy to announce the initial version of an autofix feature. You can directly use the "--fix" argument to let UI5 linter apply fixes to your code automatically.

Feature

In our initial autofix version, UI5 linter can replace the deprecated usage of UI5 globals with our recommended way of importing the respective module and using the local name in your code.

Let’s try it out! In our application we have used the deprecated global notation when creating an sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel instance:

 

sap.ui.define([
  "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
], (Controller) => {
  "use strict";

  return Controller.extend("ui5.app.controller.Home", {
    onInit() {
      this.getView().setModel(new sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel({
        achievement: "UI5 linter Autofix has landed"
      }));
    }
  });
});

 

Now, let’s execute UI5 linter to see whether it detects the deprecated usage of the JSON model. Simply call "ui5lint" in a shell of your choice.

 

> ui5lint
UI5 linter report:

/ui5-app/webapp/controller/Home.controller.js
  8:41 error Access of global variable 'sap' (sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel)  no-globals

1 problems (1 errors, 0 warnings)
  Run 'ui5lint --fix' to resolve all auto-fixable problems

Note: Run 'ui5lint --details' to get more information on the findings

 

Got it! UI5 linter has found the issue. We could fix the issue on our own or we can let UI5 linter do the job for us by executing "ui5lint --fix".

 

> ui5lint --fix
UI5 linter report:


0 problems (0 errors, 0 warnings)

 

Yay! UI5 linter says it was able to fix my code. Let’s see whether UI5 linter is right:

 

sap.ui.define([
  "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
  "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
], (Controller, JSONModel) => {
  "use strict";

  return Controller.extend("ui5.app.controller.Home", {
    onInit() {
      this.getView().setModel(new JSONModel({
        achievement: "UI5 linter Autofix has landed"
      }));
    }
  });
});

 

There are plenty of other deprecation types waiting to be fixed automatically by UI5 linter, and our team puts a huge focus on giving you an update here soon. See our roadmap https://github.com/SAP/ui5-linter/issues/323 to get an overview of our planned upcoming features.

entry.title: Exciting Opportunity for SAP Partners: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative - Cycle 1, 2025
entry.content:

This is a unique opportunity for you to collaborate with SAP and help shape the future of our products. This is your chance to make a real impact and contribute valuable insights to the development of SAP's upcoming products, features, and applications. Don't miss out – register by March 28, 2025, to join the current projects open to SAP partners. We look forward to hearing your perspectives and ideas on the following initiatives:

We believe collaboration is at the heart of innovation. By working together with our customers and partners, we can reshape the future of SAP products and transform the way businesses operate.

Don't wait – register today on influence.sap.com and join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Your Familiar SAP Menu, Now in Fiori! Unlock Easy Access to Your Favorite Transactions
entry.content:
👋 Remember how we talked about accessing your familiar SAP GUI transactions within the modern Fiori Launchpad? Well, here's a key piece of the puzzle: enabling the SAP Easy Access Menu in Fiori!

Think of the SAP Easy Access Menu (also known as the SAP Menu or User Menu) as the traditional way you navigated through SAP, with all those folders and transaction codes you might be used to.

The great news is that you can actually bring this familiar menu structure into your Fiori Launchpad! By doing a bit of behind-the-scenes configuration (like the trust relationships and activated services we discussed earlier), you can make your User Menu and the main SAP Menu appear right within Fiori's App Finder.

Why is this so helpful?

Easy Navigation: If you're used to navigating SAP using the traditional menus, this makes finding your way around in Fiori much simpler.
All Your Tools in One Place: You can access both the new Fiori apps and your familiar SAP GUI transactions from a single, unified interface.
Smooth Transition: For those transitioning to Fiori, having the familiar menu structure available can make the learning curve much less steep.
So, in addition to the shiny new Fiori apps, you can also have your trusty old SAP menus right at your fingertips within the Fiori Launchpad's App Finder! It's all about making your SAP experience as smooth and efficient as possible.

Have you enabled your SAP Easy Access Menu in Fiori? What are your thoughts on having both the old and the new in one place? Share your experiences in the comments below! 👇

entry.title: Ariba Order Confirmation Deviation Workflow in SAP S/4HANA – A Developer’s Guide
entry.content:
In today's dynamic supply chain environment, effective collaboration between buyers and suppliers is crucial. SAP Ariba Supply Chain Collaboration (SCC) enhances this collaboration by integrating seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA, enabling efficient management of processes like order confirmations, especially when deviations occur.

When suppliers confirm POs with changes in price, quantity, or delivery dates, businesses need control before those updates reach S/4. This post presents a flexible, workflow-driven solution to efficiently manage such deviations and ensure smooth procurement operations.

Business Need

When suppliers confirm purchase orders (POs) in SAP Ariba SCC, they might propose changes to:

  • Price → Requires Buyer approval

  • Quantity → Requires MRP Controller approval

  • Delivery Date → Managed via Ariba SCC configuration

  • Rejection of PO Items → Routed based on predefined workflows

To ensure these changes are systematically reviewed before being updated in SAP S/4HANA, implementing a custom deviation workflow is essential.

 

Process Overview

Part 1: IDOC Handling & Deviation Check

The process initiates when Ariba sends a confirmation back to S/4HANA. The system then:

  1. Receives the Inbound IDOC (ARBCIG_ORDRSP).

  2. Checks for Deviations in price or quantity or rejection.

  3. Triggers the Appropriate Workflow based on the type of deviation detected.

Flowchart:

 

 

Note: The above image illustrates the flow from receiving the IDOC to triggering the necessary workflows based on detected deviations.

 

Part 2: Workflow for Approvals (Price, Quantity, Rejection)

Depending on the deviation type, the system:

  1. Routes the Workflow to the appropriate approver (Buyer, MRP Controller, or designated Approver).

  2. Allows the Approver to Approve or Reject the proposed changes via Fiori or SAP Inbox.

  3. Updates the PO Accordingly based on the approval decision and sends the update back to Ariba.

Flowchart:

Note: This image showcases the approval process for different deviation types, ensuring clarity in workflow routing.

Technical Implementation Highlights

Integration with SAP Ariba SCC

SAP Ariba SCC is an extension of the Ariba Network that enhances buyers' ability to collaborate with suppliers on direct materials in supply-chain tasks like planning, procurement, and invoicing. It allows for real-time collaborative exchanges of planning/forecasting, purchasing, inventory fulfillment, and payment transactions between buyers and suppliers, improving traceability and end-to-end visibility of supply chain operations.

Key Features:

  • Real-Time Visibility: Access current information on inventory, orders, and shipments, enabling informed decision-making and swift responses to supply chain changes.

  • Order Management: Create, track, and manage purchase orders and shipment notifications, streamlining order processing and ensuring accurate fulfillment.

  • Collaborative Forecasting: Enable joint forecasting and planning with suppliers, enhancing demand prediction accuracy and reducing discrepancies.

For more detailed information, refer to the SAP Ariba Supply Chain Collaboration Overview.

Integration with SAP S/4HANA

The integration between SAP Ariba SCC and SAP S/4HANA is facilitated through the SAP Ariba Cloud Integration Gateway, which serves as middleware, allowing for seamless data exchange between the two systems. This integration ensures that supplier data, order details, and inventory information are synchronized across systems, providing a unified view of supply chain activities.

For more details on the integration process, see Implementing the Key Configuration Tasks for Supply Chain Collaboration.

Custom Enhancements

  • User Exit: EXIT_SAPLEINM_007 (MM06E001) – Handles incoming IDOC checks.

  • Business Object: ZSTPPOVARI – Triggers workflows.

  • Custom Tables:

    • ZTSTP_PO_VARIANC: Captures deviation per PO line.

    • ZTSTP_REJECTCODE: Defines routing rules based on rejection reasons.

  • Status Handling: IDOC is set to status 72 when a deviation is detected.

Workflow Actions

  • Custom Workflow Templates: Based on Task 8267 for:

    • Price Deviation: Routed to Buyer.

    • Quantity Deviation: Routed to MRP Controller.

    • Rejection: Routed based on reason code.

  • Approval Platforms: Workflows are actionable via SAP GUI or Fiori.

  • Notifications: Emails and Fiori UI contain buttons for Approve and Reject actions.

  • Decision Logging: All decisions are logged in PO item texts and sent back to Ariba.

For more information on configuring deviation approvals, refer to Configurations for Deviation Approval.

Additional checks 

  • Fallback Approvers: If no email/SAP ID is found, use default via TVARVC configuration.

  • Auto-Cancel Workflows: When a PO line is deleted or closed before approval.

  • Retry/Error Handling: If the PO is locked, implement retry logic (up to 20 times) before failure.

  • Mandatory Comments: On rejection, users must enter a reason.

  • Net Price Sync: Ensures updated values post-approval are written to the PO.

 

In conclusion, this custom solution offers robust control over supplier-initiated changes in Ariba, ensuring transparency, traceability, and proper approval routing within SAP S/4HANA. By combining IDOC enhancements, workflow automation, and clear notifications, it delivers a scalable, developer-friendly approach that supports both operational accuracy and compliance. It also empowers procurement teams to make informed decisions while maintaining seamless integration between Ariba SCC and S/4HANA.

For more detailed guidance on implementing these configurations, see Workflows for Order Confirmation Deviation Approval.

entry.title: Dynamic Theme Change in SAP UI5 Applications.
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I’m excited to share a blog on a topic that’s both practical and increasingly important in modern application development: Dynamic Theme Change in SAP UI5 Applications.

In today’s digital landscape, user experience plays a crucial role in the success of any application. One of the key aspects of a great user experience is personalization—allowing users to customize the look and feel of the application to suit their preferences. A common feature that users expect is the ability to switch between Light and Dark modes seamlessly.

In this blog, I’ll walk you through a step-by-step guide on how to implement dynamic theme switching in your SAP UI5 applications. Whether you’re building a new app or enhancing an existing one, this feature will not only improve usability but also make your application more accessible and visually appealing.

We’ll cover:

  • Why dynamic theme switching matters and its benefits.
  • How to implement it using Toggle Buttons, JSON Models, and dynamic updates.
  • Best practices to ensure a smooth and user-friendly experience.

By the end of this blog, you’ll have a clear understanding of how to add this feature to your UI5 applications and make them more engaging for your users.

Let’s dive in!

Why Dynamic Theme Switching?

Before we jump into the implementation, let’s take a moment to understand why dynamic theme switching is important:

  1. User Preference: Some users prefer Light mode during the day and Dark mode at night to reduce eye strain.
  2. Accessibility: Dark mode can improve readability for users with visual impairments.
  3. Modern Design: Supporting Light and Dark modes aligns your application with modern design trends and enhances its overall appeal.

Prerequisites

To follow along with this guide, you’ll need:

  • Basic knowledge of SAP UI5.
  • A running SAP UI5 project (you can create one using SAP Business Application Studio or any IDE).
  • Familiarity with JavaScript and XML views.

Step-by-Step Implementation

Now, let’s get into the implementation. We’ll break it down into simple steps:

  1. Setting Up the Project

If you don’t already have a UI5 project, create one using SAP Business Application Studio or your preferred IDE. For this blog, we’ll assume you have a basic project ready.

 

  1. Adding a Toggle Button for Theme Switching

The first step is to add a Toggle Button to your UI. This button will allow users to switch between Light and Dark modes.

 <ToggleButton
                id="toggleButton"
                icon="sap-icon://light-mode" 
                pressed="false"
                press=".onThemeSwitch"
                width="50px"
                class="sapUiSmallMargin"/>

     2. Implementing Dynamic Theme Switching

Next, we’ll add the logic to handle theme switching in the controller. We’ll use the sap.ui.getCore().applyTheme() method to dynamically change the theme.

onThemeSwitch: function (oEvent) {
            const oToggleButton = oEvent.getSource();
            const bIsDarkMode = oToggleButton.getPressed();
            const sNewTheme = bIsDarkMode ? "sap_horizon_dark" : "sap_horizon";
            sap.ui.getCore().applyTheme(sNewTheme);
            oToggleButton.setIcon(bIsDarkMode ? "sap-icon://dark-mode" : "sap-icon://light-mode");
            const oPage = this.byId("page"); 
            oPage.setTitle(bIsDarkMode ? "Dark Mode" : "Light Mode");
            MessageToast.show(`Switched to ${bIsDarkMode ? "Dark" : "Light"} Mode`);
        }

    3. Updating the Page Title, Toggle Button Icons and Button Title Dynamically

To make the UI more intuitive, we’ll update the Page title, Button Icons and Button title dynamically based on the selected theme.

sap.ui.getCore().applyTheme("sap_horizon");
            this.getView().attachAfterInit(function () {
                const oToggleButton = this.byId("toggleButton");
                const oPage = this.byId("page"); 
                const sCurrentTheme = sap.ui.getCore().getConfiguration().getTheme();
                const bIsDarkMode = sCurrentTheme.includes("dark");
                oToggleButton.setPressed(bIsDarkMode);
                oToggleButton.setIcon(bIsDarkMode ? "sap-icon://dark-mode" : "sap-icon://light-mode");
                oPage.setTitle(bIsDarkMode ? "Dark Mode" : "Light Mode");
            }.bind(this));

    4. Binding JSON Models to Tables

Finally, we’ll bind a JSON Model to a table to display some sample data. This will demonstrate how the theme change affects the entire application, including data tables.

const oData = {
                employees: [
                    { id: "001", name: "Ranishree", age: 24, city: "Mandya", salary: 27000 },
                    { id: "002", name: "John Doe", age: 30, city: "New York", salary: 50000 },
                    { id: "003", name: "Jane Smith", age: 28, city: "London", salary: 45000 },
                    { id: "004", name: "Alice Johnson", age: 35, city: "Berlin", salary: 60000 }
                ]
            };
            const oModel = new JSONModel(oData);
            this.getView().setModel(oModel, "employeesModel");

 

<Table items="{employeesModel>/employees}">
    <columns>
        <Column><Text text="Id"/></Column>
        <Column><Text text="Name"/></Column>
        <Column><Text text="Age"/></Column>
        <Column><Text text="City"/></Column>
        <Column><Text text="Salary"/></Column>
    </columns>
    <items>
        <ColumnListItem>
            <cells>
                <Text text="{employeesModel>id}"/>
                <Text text="{employeesModel>name}"/>
                <Text text="{employeesModel>age}"/>
                <Text text="{employeesModel>city}"/>
                <Text text="{employeesModel>salary}"/>
            </cells>
        </ColumnListItem>
    </items>
</Table>

 

The Output will be

 

 

 

 

Best Practices

Here are some tips to ensure a smooth implementation:

  1. Add Icons: Use icons like sap-icon://lightbulb for Light mode and sap-icon://moon for Dark mode to make the Toggle Button more intuitive.
  2. Test Thoroughly: Ensure your application looks great and functions well in both Light and Dark modes.

Conclusion

Dynamic theme switching is a simple yet powerful feature that can significantly enhance the user experience of your SAP UI5 applications. By following the steps outlined in this blog, you can easily implement this feature and make your applications more engaging and accessible.

I hope you found this guide helpful! If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to leave a comment below. Happy coding!

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – March 2025 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Greetings and welcome to the March edition of our SAP Build Code release communications blog post! We are thrilled to introduce a lot of innovative features and improvements that are set to enhance your development experience.

Dive into our new high productivity experience for CAP Java projects, designed to simplify and speed up your application development journey. Learn how we support the use of runtime capabilities in Full-Stack applications, the creation of UI applications via our AI copilot Joule, and the development of MDK-based projects utilizing Joule. Additionally, explore the new possibility to bind a database project directly to a native SAP HANA Cloud database tenant. In addition to these exciting updates, we’ve curated a collection of informative blog posts that delve into various aspects of SAP Build Code.

To explore these enhancements in depth, check out the details of what’s new in this edition!

 

High Productivity Experience for CAP Java Projects

We're excited to announce that our high productivity tools now support SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) Java projects. These tools simplify and accelerate the application development lifecycle, enabling a visual approach that reduces complexity and speeds up custom application delivery.

Creating a CAP Java Project from the Lobby

You may have noticed the new Java Development Stack option when creating a new Full-Stack project from the SAP Build Lobby. Additionally, when creating a new project from the Project Overview, you'll have the option to select between a "Full-Stack with Productivity Tools" or a "Full-Stack Java" project. Once your CAP Java project is created, you'll find that the Storyboard, with all its powerful features, is ready to use.

Creating a CAP Java Project from the Project Overview

Additionally, other high-productivity features are available for your CAP Java project:

  • Easily add OData services as external resources to your CAP Java projects via the Service Center.
  • Moreover, when creating a new run configuration, a runner specifically for CAP Java applications can be selected.

Creating a Run Configuration for a CAP Java Project

 

Enhancing Your Full-Stack Applications with SAP BTP Runtime Capabilities

Are you looking to implement best practices for using SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) runtime services and capabilities? Do you need assistance in integrating these services into your projects? We have great news for you: we now offer support for using runtime capabilities in your Full-Stack applications.

Starting the Runtime Capabilities Editor

In the storyboard, you'll find a new button labeled "Runtime Capabilities." Clicking this button opens the Runtime Capabilities Editor, where you can access detailed information about each capability and the SAP BTP services it relies on. To install a capability, simply select the corresponding button. This will add the relevant feature to your application (after updating your service.cds file).

The Runtime Capabilities Editor

Currently, the following capabilities are supported:

  • Audit Logging
  • Telemetry
  • Attachments
  • Change Tracking

 

Generating UI Applications with the Help of Joule

Discover how to leverage generative AI to assist you in adding a UI to your existing CAP applications.  This is made possible through our new /add-ui command. With this command, you can effortlessly generate SAP Fiori or mobile UI applications for your existing CAP application, assisted by our generative AI copilot Joule. Moreover, this functionality can also be activated directly from the storyboard by clicking on the Joule icon. To utilize this feature, ensure that the corresponding backend services are defined and available.

Adding a UI with the Help of Joule

 

Create an MDK Based Project Using Joule

We're delighted to share a new feature in SAP Build Code! You can now generate a fully working Mobile Development Kit (MDK) project using Joule. This integration brings you a streamlined and efficient way to kickstart your mobile development projects. 

Creating an MDK Project with Joule

 

SAP HANA Cloud Database Tenant Development

With the Q4 2024 release, the SAP HANA Database within SAP HANA Cloud introduced native multi-tenancy on database level. Customer data is managed in database tenants which are sharing the same database instance but are logically separated. For more information please check out the introduction blog post.

When working on SAP HANA Cloud projects in SAP Build Code it is now possible to bind a database project to a native HANA Cloud database tenant. The prerequisite is that a service instance of type hana-cloud|hana-multitenancy-admin is available in the target space. Then a project can be bound as usual via the new option “Bind to an SAP HANA Cloud multi-tenant service instance”.

Binding a Database Project

The first step is to select an available multi-tenant service instance. After that the target SAP HANA Cloud database ID can be entered. Next, an existing tenant can be chosen, or a new tenant can be created that will contain the HDI container used for development. After selecting the tenant, a container within the tenant can be chosen or a new container can be created. After that the project is bound to a tenant container and can be used for development as usual.

 

Further readings & Events

  • Speed up your Development with SAP Build

We are excited to share some remarkable insights from the latest study conducted by a 3rd party Technology Analyst – Gigaom, which delves into the impact of SAP Build on development speed. Today, businesses have to rapidly adjust to shifting market conditions and customer demands. Thus, agility and efficiency are more crucial than ever. The study reveals how SAP Build is enabling businesses to achieve greater speed and agility in their development processes, ultimately driving innovation and success.

Join us as we explore the key findings and implications of this groundbreaking study: GigaOm Benchmark Study: SAP Build Speed of Development.

  • Introducing: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 7: UI Design and Technology for Web and Mobile

Discover the latest innovations of our UI technologies that are simplifying the design, development, and integration of UIs for both Web and mobile platforms. In his series of blog posts my colleague Thomas Reiss gives an overview of user experience innovations in the cloud from the last six months. In his seventh and final blog post, he explains how to build Joule agents and how AI can facilitate the process of developing UIs for SAP Fiori elements and freestyle SAPUI5. Plus, learn how to transform a sketch into an entire application complete with backend data models, services, and the UI! In addition to AI-driven innovations, this article provides a comprehensive look at further news in Web and mobile UI design and development.

Explore the full details here: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 7.

  • Announcing Code Connect 2025!

Get ready to learn, collaborate, and drive innovation at Code Connect 2025 bringing together three events in one week and one location: UI5con, re>≡CAP, and HANA Tech Con! Whether you're a seasoned expert or new to the SAP community, this event is your opportunity to make meaningful connections and become part of the driving force behind digital transformation. Mark your calendars for July 08-10, 2025, and be sure to join Code Connect 2025 in St. Leon-Rot, Germany, or online from anywhere in the world.

Explore everything you need to know at code-connect.dev.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: SAP Gardener Agents: Cultivating a Thriving Digital Ecosystem 🌱🚀
entry.content:
Imagine an SAP Joule Agent that gardens for you. It maintains your integration flows, improves your BEx queries, looks for the latest Fiori UI elements for your apps. Running in the background, this agent reads through logs and script files in your BTP landscape, and suggests improvements for it.

Our team is working to create this agent. More practical details coming soon...


Welcome to a new era of SAP management—a proactive, behind-the-scenes advisor that nurtures your digital garden to perfection. Imagine having a dedicated tool that not only monitors your integration flows, BW, and Fiori apps but also continuously optimizes, updates, and safeguards them. Let’s explore this powerful concept and see how concrete examples for different SAP domains can transform your digital landscape!

Why Embrace the SAP Gardener? 🌟

  • Proactive Maintenance: Regular health checks prevent issues before they impact your operations.

  • Best Practices: Continuous updates to guidelines, glossaries, and scripts keep your system in top shape.

  • Efficiency & ROI: Automation minimizes manual tasks, reduces downtime, and drives cost savings.

  • Scalability: The concept scales across multiple SAP solutions and industries—from manufacturing to finance.

Concrete Examples of SAP Gardeners

1. Integration Flow Gardener 🌐

Purpose:

  • Manage and optimize integration flows within the SAP Integration Suite.

What It Does:

  • Weekly Health Checks: Automatically scans API integration flows to identify bottlenecks.

  • Performance Optimization: Adjusts settings and prioritizes data flows for maximum efficiency.

  • Security Monitoring: Proactively secures integration points and alerts the team to any vulnerabilities.

Example Scenario:
Imagine your organization handles multiple data integrations between SAP modules. The Integration Flow Gardener runs a weekly diagnostic, detects an inefficient data route slowing down transactions, and automatically reroutes the flow for faster performance—all while logging changes for audit and future reference. 🔄💡
It would leverage the standard APIs : https://api.sap.com/package/CloudIntegrationAPI/odata

2. BW Gardener 📊

Purpose:

  • Ensure SAP BW (Business Warehouse) processes run smoothly and data integrity is maintained.

What It Does:

  • Data Quality Assurance: Continuously checks for inconsistencies in data loads and reporting.

  • Query Performance Tuning: Optimizes complex BEx queries to speed up report generation.

  • Cleanup & Maintenance: Removes redundant data scripts and updates reports to align with new business metrics.

Example Scenario:
Your BW system processes vast amounts of data for monthly financial reports. The BW Gardener identifies an outdated script causing delayed data refreshes and replaces it with an optimized version. This proactive tweak not only speeds up the report generation but also ensures that decision-makers always have access to accurate, real-time data. 🔍📈
The agent would read requests from "RSA1"

3. Fiori Gardener 📱

Purpose:

  • Enhance the performance and user experience of SAP Fiori apps.

What It Does:

  • UI/UX Optimization: Monitors app performance and ensures adherence to Fiori design guidelines.

  • Responsive Testing: Regularly checks apps on various devices to guarantee a consistent user experience.

  • Feedback Integration: Collects user feedback to fine-tune app functionalities and interfaces.

Example Scenario:
A popular Fiori app experiences occasional slow load times during peak hours. The Fiori Gardener performs an analysis, pinpoints the issue to an overloaded data call, and implements a caching mechanism to improve responsiveness. The result? Smoother navigation, happier users, and a more efficient app overall. 📲✨
It would read the repositories of these apps, and SAP BTP Cloud Foundry logs

Key Takeaways 📝

  • Continuous Improvement: Regular monitoring and automated maintenance keep systems running at peak performance.

  • Proactive Issue Prevention: Weekly checks and real-time alerts help mitigate risks before they escalate.

  • Cross-Functional Benefits: Whether it’s integration flows, BW, or Fiori apps, a tailored gardener approach optimizes each area uniquely.

  • Enhanced ROI: Reduced downtime and manual intervention translate into significant cost savings and better resource allocation.

Next Steps 🚀

  1. Reflect: Consider which areas of your SAP ecosystem could benefit most from a dedicated "gardener" approach.

  2. Experiment: Start with one module (e.g., Integration Flow Gardener) to test the concept in a controlled environment.

  3. Collaborate: Share your experiences and insights with your team or on professional platforms like LinkedIn to learn and grow collectively.

  4. Innovate: Explore automation tools and best practices that can integrate seamlessly with your SAP systems, driving both efficiency and innovation.

entry.title: AI in My Home enhances efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making for SAP users
entry.content:
Business AI offers numerous benefits that can transform organisations by enhancing efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making. AI allows business users to focus on more strategic, creative, or high-value work, increasing productivity. Over the past few years, we have seen the growth of AI within SAP products, including My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. In this blog, I will discuss how AI enhances SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition My Home and look at future* AI - driven features.  

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release, My Home introduced personalised app recommendations powered by SAP Business AI. By analysing business users' history, behaviours, location, and authorisations, it recommended personalised applications to the business user. These recommendations make business users more efficient by allowing them to add these recommended applications to their app favourites. It also introduces them to applications that they may not be aware of which in return improves their productivity.

 

My Home Intelligent App Recommendations

 

Based on these intelligent app recommendations, My Home now intelligently generates Insights cards from these applications and pre delivers them for business users. Insights cards display valuable business information about business processes. They provide analytical information at glance so that business users can take action immediately instead of having to search for information. This also makes interactions with SAP systems more intuitive for new users, who may know what they need to do, but not necessarily know what application they need to find the business data. 

 

Intelligent generation of pre-delivered Insights cards

 

With the success of these intelligent recommendations, we are now focused on how we can further enhance the user experience with the help of AI. With the 2408 release we pre delivered Insights cards to business users but received feedback that we could improve the experience of adding more Insights cards to My Home. Traditionally, Insights cards are added from supporting applications. Identifying the applications that can provide the information that business users require can be time consuming. Not only that, the type of applications that support adding Insights cards can be difficult to find. The business user not only has to identify the application, but they also have to apply certain filters to the data to ensure that the Insights card displays the information they require for it to be useful. We are currently working on a solution where business users can create Insights cards using natural language. They can describe their desired information without having to find the appropriate application and navigating to it.  

 

My Home Smart Personalisation feature

 

Influence My Home Smart Personalisation feature 

This Smart Card generation AI driven feature is available for Beta Testing with the 2502 release. At SAP, we're dedicated to delivering the best possible experience for our customers. That's why we're inviting you to participate in our Beta Testing Program , where you can provide valuable feedback on this new feature and help us refine it  before its official release. By taking part in beta testing, you'll have the opportunity to explore the latest functionality before it's available to the wider customer base, and your input can directly impact the quality and usability of the features. 

 

* Roadmap statement: Please note that some of the content in this blog is about unreleased solutions from SAP. SAP reserves the right to change its development priorities at any time, so please do not make any purchase decisions based on what you read here.

entry.title: Announcement: Removal of Belize Theme of SAP Fiori
entry.content:
Executive summary

  • Automatic move to Horizon theme in SAP Fiori
  • Action is needed for custom themes based on Belize.

Themes in SAP Fiori

SAP Fiori is a design system that provides a consistent user experience for SAP software across platforms and devices. It is at the core of SAP’s product experience and design strategy. An essential element defining many aspects of the visual design is the theme. The latest Horizon theme is an evolution of the SAP Fiori design system and offers a modern, fresh look, and an improved user experience helping users to focus on their daily work. 

Belize theme removal

A previous version of Horizon is the Belize theme, introduced with SAP Fiori 2.0. Belize is deprecated as of SAPUI5 1.120 (see Deprecated Themes and Libraries) and SAP recommends to move to the current Horizon theme.

 

Existing usages of Belize will be automatically redirected to the Horizon theme.
With SAPUI5/OpenUI5 1.136 the UI5 controls will not work any longer with the Belize theme.

For more details on Belize in SAP S/4HANA and other products, please see Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori themes Belize and Blue Crystal from @ThomasReiss. Other UI technologies are affected in the same way:

  • Web Dynpro ABAP: SAP_UI 8.16 & SAP_BASIS 9.16
  • SAP GUI for HTML: SAP Kernel 9.16 used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508
  • WebClient UI: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508

With this focus on the Horizon theme SAP can deliver innovations faster. Also, runtime performance is expected to gain from a smaller CSS payload.

Custom themes based on Belize

Using the UI theme designer customers can apply their own branding by adapting a standard SAP Fiori theme: Customers may replace the logo or change the main branding colors of the UI by replacing the default brand and base colors of the theme. When creating a custom theme, an SAP standard theme (such as Morning Horizon) is selected as base theme.

Existing custom themes based on Belize need to be migrated before a mentioned UI technology version is used. Details on this migration are given in Migrate Your Themes with Ease and SAP Help: Migrating Themes on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment.

entry.title: Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal have been deprecated for many years. Now the time has come to optimize our UI technology by removing these themes. We plan to remove them in the next releases of products which allow users to choose multiple themes, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA (on-premise) and SAP Integrated Business Planning.

Over the years SAP Fiori has evolved, introducing new visual themes along the way – most recently the Horizon theme. The Horizon theme not only looks modern and appealing, it also provides the highest level of support for accessibility, as well as the most flexibility for customers to create their own custom themes.

Horizon is the visual theme used by all our products in the SAP Business Suite. Figures 1 – 3 below show some examples of Horizon in our products.

We strongly recommend that all users use the Horizon theme to ensure an optimal and consistent user experience.

 

Removal of Belize and Blue Crystal

The visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal will be removed from SAP products with their next releases, starting in the second half of 2025. This means that the upcoming releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Integrated Business Planning, as well as further products which currently include one or both of these themes, will no longer provide them as options.

After upgrading, existing usages of Belize or Blue Crystal will be automatically redirected to the Horizon theme.

If you have created your own custom theme based on Belize or Blue Crystal using the UI theme designer, you will need to migrate this to an available theme. Our strong recommendation is to base the new version on the Horizon theme.

For more information about the UI technologies affected, and how to migrate custom themes, have a look at this blog post by Oliver Graeff:

  • Announcement: Removal of Belize theme of SAP Fiori.

Additional Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Important: If you use a custom theme and have set up the UI Theme Designer connection to your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with a communication arrangement on SAP Business Technology Platform Neo, make sure to move it to Cloud Foundry before you upgrade. Otherwise the automatic redirect to Horizon will not work. Also, future innovations will require the communication arrangement to use Cloud Foundry, so doing this move makes sense anyway. Here is a link to the documentation for this:

  • Integrating UI Theme Designer – SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environment.

 

Examples of SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon

You can see some examples for Horizon in figures 1 – 3: for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start, for SAP S/4HANA Cloud (the images show SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502) and SAP SuccessFactors. For further examples, have a look at these blog posts:

  • SAP Fiori Update: More Products with Horizon, Entry Point strategy, UX news for SAP S/4HANA and More.
  • SAP User Experience Update: Yet More Products with the New Look driven by SAP Fiori.
  • Improvements Coming to the Horizon Visual Theme for SAP Fiori.
  • SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more.
  • SAP UX Latest 2024 Innovations in Public Cloud: Joule, GenAI and More.
  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More) – along with parts 2 – 7 which are linked therein.

Figure 1: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start. ALT Text: the image shows a screenshot of SAP Start with the Horizon theme, three iPhones with Horizon (one dark and one light showing SAP Mobile start, one showing an iPhone start screen with a notification from SAP Mobile STart ), and an Apple Watch with a notification from SAP Mobile Start and Horizon.

Figure 2: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. ALT Text: an image showing two sceens with Morning Horizon (the My Home page and Monitor Purchase Order Items analytical list page) and two screens with the same apps with Evening Horizon, plus the text “Also available for accessibility: high contrast light and dark themes”.

Figure 3: Examples of SAP SuccessFactors applications with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: Three examples showing both web and mobile: on the left the Home Page, in the middle Team Goals, on the right Opportunity Marketplace showing many cards, each displaying an opportunity.

To find out more about the Horizon theme, have a look at the SAP Fiori for web design guidelines:

  • Theming.
